signals.idea:
Replace the preview panel in the video editor top pane with a shots browser panel (on all pages except the main video travel tool page).

## What to build

A horizontal-scrolling shots panel in the top pane of the video editor that:

1. Shows all project shots in a compact grid (4-5 per row, tightly packed)
2. Each shot shows its thumbnail/preview (final video thumbnail if available, otherwise first image)
3. Horizontal scroll to browse all shots
4. Search/filter by shot name
5. Shots are draggable onto the timeline (already have shot drag type + SortableShotItem drag support)
6. When a clip is selected on the timeline, the corresponding shot is highlighted in the panel
7. Can filter to show only shots related to the current selection
8. When dropped on timeline: if shot has a final video → show video, otherwise → show images

## Existing components to reuse
- ShotListDisplay (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/ShotListDisplay.tsx) — main shot grid
- SortableShotItem (src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/SortableShotItem.tsx) — draggable shot card with shot drag data
- VideoShotDisplay / VideoShotDisplayParts — shot card rendering
- useShotFinalVideos — final video data
- useShots/ShotsContext — shot data
- The shot drag type already exists in shared DnD (setShotDragData/getShotDropData)

## Layout
- The top pane currently shows PreviewPanel (Remotion preview)
- On pages OTHER than the main video travel tool page, replace this with the shots panel
- Keep it compact — this is a browse/drag-source panel, not a full editor
- Consider a toggle to switch between preview and shots panel

## Highlighting/filtering
- selectedClipIds → meta → assetGenerationMap → generationIds → find shots containing those generations
- Highlight matching shots with a ring/border treatment
- Optional filter toggle: 'Show related shots only'

## Shot drop behavior (already mostly built)
- useExternalDrop already handles shot drops
- It checks finalVideoMap — if shot has video, creates video clip; otherwise creates image clips
- Creates a pinned group automatically

## Key files to modify
- src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx — layout, conditionally show shots panel vs preview
- New: src/tools/video-editor/components/ShotsPanel.tsx (or similar) — the compact shots browser
- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx — expose selectedShotIds for highlighting
- May need a compact variant of ShotListDisplay or a new simplified component

unresolved_flags.concern: Video editor shell: the plan keys the preview-vs-shots switch off `/tools/video-editor`, but in this repository the main Video Travel page is `/tools/travel-between-images` and the embedded editor pane only exists off the standalone editor route, so the exception logic is aimed at the wrong surface.

Shot drop behavior: the plan assumes shot drops already create a video clip when a final video exists, but the current drop handler only registers image generations and persists a pinned group in images mode.

Preview host lifecycle: replacing `PreviewPanel` alone is insufficient because `previewOverlay` is outside `PreviewPanel` and the only existing persistence test covers visible compact/full slots, not a new hidden-host path.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the routing and shell mounting paths in `src/app/routes.tsx:138-165`, `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:81-89`, and `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:291-299`. The plan assumes the only page that should keep the preview is `/tools/video-editor`, but the repository's named Video Travel page is `/tools/travel-between-images` while the standalone video editor is a separate route; using `isOnEditorPage` to gate the shots panel would therefore apply the replacement on the main travel tool page that the brief called out as the exception.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the stated shot-drop behavior claim against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts:104-189` and the existing regression in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.test.tsx:510-543`. The current shot-drop path always expands a shot into image clips and writes `mode: 'images'`; it does not consult `finalVideoMap` or create a video clip when a final video exists, so the plan leaves a required part of the approved brief unplanned.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Checked the reuse guidance against `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/ShotListDisplay.tsx:38-139` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/VideoGallery/SortableShotItem.tsx:112-131`. The brief explicitly called out these components as reuse targets, but the plan instead commits to a brand-new inline card and explicitly avoids `SortableShotItem`, which is a real divergence from the requested reuse strategy even if a lighter-weight panel may still be justified.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked where `VideoEditorShell` runs in each mode. `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:293-298` always mounts the shell in `mode="full"` on `/tools/video-editor`, while `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:87-89` and `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:152-155` only mount `mode="compact"` off that route. Because of that split, the plan's `!isOnEditorPage` branch for the standard full layout is effectively dead in this repository and does not describe the real runtime surface that needs to change.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the existing top-pane structure in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:344-390` and `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:501-503`. The preview time/readout and render controls are rendered by `previewOverlay` outside `PreviewPanel`, so swapping only `PreviewPanel` for a shots browser would still leave preview-specific chrome floating over the new panel unless the plan also relocates or redesigns that overlay.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: Checked the hidden-preview assumption against the current persistence implementation in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:393-405` and the only regression test in `src/tools/video-editor/components/__tests__/PreviewPersistence.test.tsx:264-285`. The repository currently preserves one Remotion player by moving `previewHostEl` between the compact and full visible slots; the plan's new hidden host/toggle path changes that lifecycle but does not spell out how the existing single-node invariant is preserved, so the playback-continuity assumption is still under-specified.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked all call sites that surface the preview area. The repository has two distinct shells for this concept: the standalone editor's full layout in `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:293-298` and the embedded editor pane's compact layout in `src/features/editor/components/EditorPaneTab.tsx:152-155`. The user-visible `all pages except the main video travel tool page` behavior is therefore broader than a single `PreviewPanel` swap and requires explicit handling of route exceptions plus the compact tab layout, not just the full-layout top-left cell.

Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: Checked related timeline-selection infrastructure in `src/tools/video-editor/components/VideoEditorShell.tsx:62-100` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts:45-64`. `VideoEditorShell` already receives `selectedClipIds` and the materialized timeline data from context, so the plan's proposed `TimelineEditor.tsx` change to expose `selectedShotIds` is unnecessary scope expansion rather than missing infrastructure in the repository.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 19.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 11.

items.criterion: On pages other than /tools/video-editor, the top pane shows the ShotsPanel instead of PreviewPanel
On /tools/video-editor, the PreviewPanel (Remotion preview) is shown as before — no regression
Shots are rendered in a compact horizontal-scrolling grid showing thumbnails and names
Shots are draggable from the panel onto the timeline using existing setShotDragData format
Dropping a shot on the timeline creates clips correctly (video if available, images otherwise) via existing useExternalDrop
When clips are selected on the timeline, corresponding shots are highlighted in the panel
Search input filters shots by name
'Related only' filter toggle shows only shots matching the current clip selection
Remotion preview playback continues to function (time sync, play/pause) even when ShotsPanel is displayed
User can toggle back to Remotion preview from the shots panel
No new dependencies added — reuses existing DnD utilities and shot hooks
Panel handles empty state (no shots) and loading state gracefully
Condensed layout includes Shots tab or replaces Preview when not on editor page
Grid shows 4-5 shots per visible row with tight spacing
